title: The Illusion of Authentic Travel
sentences: Every traveller secretly hopes to stumble upon somewhere untouched by other tourists.
Such a place, if it ever existed, becomes compromised the moment it is discovered.
I used to chase this fantasy relentlessly, mistaking obscurity for authenticity.
Gradually, I realised authenticity has little to do with how few visitors a place attracts.
A crowded market can feel just as genuine as a quiet trail, depending on one's attention.
What matters, I have come to think, is the quality of engagement rather than exclusivity.
Ironically, my most memorable journeys involved conversations rather than scenery.
Chasing untouched destinations, I now suspect, says more about ego than curiosity.
These days I would rather linger somewhere ordinary than rush toward the supposedly pristine.
That shift in perspective has, oddly enough, made travelling far more rewarding.
